assumptions: The recognised intragroup dividend receivable/payable is a monetary item.
The question concerns hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements under IFRS 9.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge of intragroup monetary item
approaches.rationale: A recognised intragroup receivable/payable can qualify in consolidation as a hedged item when its foreign currency gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation.
conditions: The dividend has been recognised as an intragroup receivable/payable
The item creates foreign exchange gains or losses not fully eliminated on consolidation, typically because the entities have different functional currencies
Formal designation, documentation and hedge effectiveness requirements are met at inception

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge of forecast intragroup transaction
approaches.rationale: This approach applies only to a highly probable forecast intragroup transaction whose foreign currency risk will affect consolidated profit or loss, whereas a recognised dividend receivable is no longer a forecast transaction.
conditions: Only relevant before the dividend is recognised as a receivable/payable
The forecast intragroup transaction must be highly probable
The foreign currency risk must affect consolidated profit or loss

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale: A net investment hedge applies to foreign currency risk on net assets of a foreign operation, not to settlement of an intragroup dividend receivable.
conditions: The hedged item must be an amount of net assets of a foreign operation included in the consolidated financial statements
The hedge must satisfy the IFRS 9 designation, documentation and effectiveness requirements for a net investment hedge
